questions.question: Explain the three main functions of an Operating System.
keyPoints: Resource Management: Manages CPU, memory, I/O devices efficiently
User Interface: Provides interface (CLI/GUI) for user interaction
Program Execution: Loads and executes application software

questions.question: What is the difference between Kernel mode and User mode? Give one example of when each mode is used.
keyPoints: Kernel mode: Privileged mode with full hardware access
User mode: Restricted mode with limited privileges
Example Kernel: Device driver execution, memory management
Example User: Running applications like browsers, editors

questions.question: Describe the bootstrap program and its role in the operating system.
keyPoints: First program executed when computer powers on
Stored in ROM or EEPROM
Initializes hardware components (CPU, memory, devices)
Loads the OS kernel into primary memory
Transfers control to the kernel to start OS operations

questions.question: What is multi-programming? How does it improve CPU utilization?
keyPoints: Technique of keeping multiple programs in memory simultaneously
When one program waits for I/O, CPU executes another program
Increases CPU utilization by minimizing idle time
Through context switching between processes

questions.question: Explain the evolution of Operating Systems from manual operations to modern systems.
keyPoints: Manual Operations: No OS, operators used front panel switches
Batch Systems: Jobs submitted in batches on punch cards
Multiprogrammed Batch: Multiple jobs in memory, increased efficiency
Time-sharing: Interactive computing for multiple users
Personal Computer: Single user OS with GUI
Distributed: Networked computers working as single system
Mobile OS: Touch-based, app-centric operating systems

questions.question: What are system calls? List any four types of system calls with examples.
keyPoints: System calls provide interface for programs to request OS services
Types: Process control (fork, exec), File management (open, read), Device management (read, write), Communication (pipe, socket)
Examples: fork() creates new process, open() accesses files, ioctl() controls devices

questions.question: Define a process. Explain the different states of a process.
keyPoints: Process: Program in execution with its own address space and resources
States: New (being created), Running (instructions executing), Waiting (blocked for I/O), Ready (waiting for CPU), Terminated (completed)
Process control block (PCB) stores process information

questions.question: Discuss the main components of an Operating System architecture. Explain how each component contributes to the functioning of the OS.
keyPoints: Kernel: Core component managing resources, process scheduling, memory management
Shell/Command Interpreter: Interface accepting and executing user commands
Device Drivers: Software enabling communication between OS and hardware devices
File System: Manages files on storage devices
System Utilities: Programs for system maintenance tasks
Application Interface (API): Allows applications to use OS services
Protection System: Ensures authorized access to resources

questions.question: Explain the different types of Operating Systems based on usage. Give one example of each.
keyPoints: Batch OS: Jobs processed in groups, no user interaction (IBM mainframe)
Time-sharing OS: Multiple users share CPU simultaneously (UNIX)
Real-time OS: Guaranteed response time for critical operations (VxWorks, RTLinux)
Network OS: Manages network resources and provides networking capabilities (Windows Server)
Distributed OS: Multiple computers work together as single system (Amoeba)
Mobile OS: Designed for mobile devices with touch interface (Android, iOS)

questions.question: What are the services provided by an Operating System to user programs? Explain any five services in detail.
keyPoints: Program Execution: Loading and running user programs
I/O Operations: Performing input/output on devices
File System Manipulation: Creating, reading, writing, deleting files
Communication: Exchange of data between processes (pipes, sockets)
Error Detection: Detecting and reporting hardware/software errors
Resource Allocation: Distributing CPU time, memory among processes
Accounting: Tracking resource usage for billing/performance
Protection: Controlling access to system resources

questions.question: Explain the difference between system software and application software with examples. Also describe how they interact with the Operating System.
keyPoints: System Software: Manages hardware, provides platform (OS, device drivers, compilers)
Application Software: Performs specific user tasks (browsers, games, word processors)
Interaction: Applications use system calls to access OS services, OS allocates resources, Device drivers mediate hardware access
Examples: Windows/Linux (system), MS Word/Chrome (application)
